name: Track 1 Execution Contract
goal: Fix the highest-risk contract contradictions first: HR hidden mismatch, Estimate summary backend blocker, and Estimate export CSV mismatch.

items.title: HR hidden-domain contract fix
items.goal: Ensure viewer and contractor do not have HR domain visibility or HR-shaped operational data when the contract says HR is hidden.

current_behavior.summary: Audit identified a semantic mismatch: contract says viewer/contractor HR is hidden, but current implementation exposes broader HR-related behavior than intended.
known_risks: viewer and contractor may still access or receive HR-shaped operational data
hidden means hidden is currently violated
AI inheritance would also be unsafe if base HR visibility is not fixed

constraints: do not introduce fake frontend-only hiding if backend still leaks HR-shaped rows
assignee names in non-HR domains may remain visible only where explicitly allowed by contract

rovno: any HR-related read hooks or summary surfaces
rovno-db: HR-related helper/policy/RPC surfaces currently reachable by non-hidden roles

acceptance_criteria: viewer direct HR route is denied cleanly
contractor direct HR route is denied cleanly
viewer receives no HR operational summary payload
contractor receives no HR operational summary payload
co_owner and owner HR behavior remains valid
no AI-usable hidden HR action or hidden HR data path remains implied by implementation
verification: manual role matrix check for viewer / contractor / co_owner / owner
rollback_notes: revert isolated HR visibility and data-path changes only
do not rollback unrelated Estimate or Procurement work

items.title: Estimate summary contract alignment
items.goal: Align backend truth and frontend behavior so estimate summary shows exactly the contract-defined client-safe fields and does not rely on fake or unavailable backend semantics.

current_behavior.summary: Audit identified a backend blocker: contract requires summary-level estimate client-facing values, but current backend truth does not cleanly provide the full summary behavior defined in contract.
known_risks: summary may show too little or inconsistent data
frontend may fake or partially infer summary fields
summary semantics drift between backend, frontend, and contract

constraints: do not invent summary semantics beyond contract
do not infer client-facing values from hidden internal values
do not ship frontend-only fake summary if backend truth cannot support it
if backend and frontend changes must be split, do backend truth first

rovno-db: estimate summary read contract / RPC / view / helper surfaces
backend truth support for summary-safe estimate values

acceptance_criteria: summary users see exactly the contract-defined estimate fields and no more
none users see only Stage, Work, Resource title, Resource type, Qty, Unit, Assignee plus timing in upper block
detail users retain full estimate financial visibility
no hidden estimate financial field leaks through summary/none paths
frontend no longer depends on unavailable or ambiguous backend summary semantics
verification: manual role x financial_visibility matrix for estimate
rollback_notes: revert estimate summary alignment changes without touching HR or CSV changes
avoid rolling back shared permission seams unless strictly necessary

items.title: Estimate export CSV contract fix
items.goal: Ensure CSV/export behavior matches the canonical contract and does not leak estimate financial detail for roles that should not have export access.

current_behavior.summary: Audit identified a contract mismatch: export remains available where the contract says it must be hidden for viewer and contractor.
known_risks: financial data may leak through export
UI and backend truth may disagree about allowed export behavior

constraints: do not redesign export system broadly
do not keep export visible while merely trimming columns if contract says hidden
do not break co_owner/owner valid export behavior
acceptance_criteria: viewer does not see or trigger estimate export
contractor does not see or trigger estimate export
co_owner can export where contract allows
owner can export where contract allows
no estimate financial leakage via CSV from hidden roles
verification: manual role check for export button visibility

rules: do not change contract semantics during implementation
if contract must change, stop and revise the contract first
prefer backend-first when frontend behavior depends on missing backend truth
keep change sets narrow and intention-pure
